title: In vitro transcription of S. pyogenes Cas9 sgRNAs from two complementary oligos
author: Stephen Floor
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.q9gdz3w

steps.title: Order two oligos that have ~20nt...
steps.instruction: Order two oligos that have ~20nt complementarity in the middle that span the guide RNA, with a T7 promoter at the 5' end. The sequence of the T7 promoter is TAATACGACTCACTATAGG. Note that the first two G's here are added onto the template. If your target sequence starts with G or GG, replace the GG at the end of the T7 promoter with the protospacer nucleotides.

steps.title: Oligo design - capital letters are the protospacer
steps.instruction: Oligo design - capital letters are the protospacer: a

steps.title: Illustration of the sgRNA in context of a target
steps.instruction: Illustration of the sgRNA in context of a target: a

steps.title: Oligo sequences
steps.instruction: Oligo sequences: Top: 5' - TAATACGACTCACTATAGG NNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNN GTTTTAGAGCTAGAAATAGCAAGTTAAAATAG - 3' Bottom: 5' - AAAAAAGCACCGACTCGGTGCCACTTTTTCAAGTTGATAACGGACTAGCCTATTTTAACTTGCTATTTCT - 3' sgRNA fwd primer: GGATCCTAATACGACTCACTATAG sgRNA rev primer: AAAAAAGCACCGACTCGG Only change the sequence of the protospacer (N's above). The bottom oligo and fwd/rev primers are always the same and only needs to be ordered once.

steps.title: Resuspend oligos to 100µM
steps.instruction: Resuspend oligos to 100µM. x nmol oligo * 10 = ul ddH 2 O to add to yield 100µM

steps.title: Set up two 100µL PCRs per sgRNA template
steps.instruction: Set up two 100µL PCRs per sgRNA template. If just making one sgRNA or a small number, use the single-reaction setup. If making many, make the master mix and add 95µL of the master mix to each tube and 5µL of the sgRNA-specific top oligo. %s %s Reagent Volume (µl) Master mix volume (µl) Number of reactions (2 * # of sgRNAs) 5x HF buffer 20 44 2 1 uM top oligo (sgRNA-specific) 2 - 1 uM bottom oligo (always the same) 2 4.4 100 uM sgRNA fwd 2 100 uM sgRNA rev 2 dNTPs 2 4.4 phusion polymerase 1 2.2 ddH2O 69 151.8

steps.title: Run a PCR with the following program
steps.instruction: Run a PCR with the following program: 98°C for 2 minutes 98°C for 30 seconds 60°C for 30 seconds 72°C for 30 seconds repeat for 30 cycles 72°C for 1 minute 4°C forever

steps.title: Pour a 2% agarose gel with...
steps.instruction: Pour a 2% agarose gel with SYBR Safe dye and using orange G dye in the loading buffer.

steps.title: Load 5µL of each PCR and...
steps.instruction: Load 5µL of each PCR and 5µL of the Lonza DNA Marker 50-1,000bp (50461). undetermined, of each PCR undetermined, Lonza DNA Marker 50-1,000bp

steps.title: Run at ~100V until the orange...
steps.instruction: Run at ~100V until the orange G band is ~2/3rds through the gel.

steps.title: Image the gel
steps.instruction: Image the gel. If there is one major band around 100 nt, perform a PCR cleanup (Qiagen) and proceed directly to in vitro transcription. If there are multiple bands, pour a preparative 2% agarose gel(s) with comb large enough to hold the entire 200µL PCR volume, run the gel and gel purify (Qiagen)

steps.title: Prepare the transcription templates from above...
steps.instruction: Prepare the transcription templates from above at ~100ng/µL or adjust the volume of template versus DEPC H 2 O in the table below. The amount of template is variable, and even 500 nanograms for a 100µL reaction should be plenty.

steps.title: Set up one of the reactions...
steps.instruction: Set up one of the reactions below depending on desired yield: %s %s Reagent 100 ul reaction 1 ml reaction 5 ml reaction Final concentration 10X transcription buffer 10 100 500 1X DTT (1 M) 2 20 50 20 mM 25 mM A/C/G/U mix 20 200 1000 5 mM Pyrophosphatase (Roche) 0.5 1 5 1 ug/ml T7 polymerase 10 100 500 100 ug/ml Template (100 ng/ul) 10 100 500 1 uM DEPC H2O 49.5 499 3 * 832 (2495) Expected yield ~50 micrograms ~500 micrograms ~2 mg Expected yield (sgRNA molarity) ~15 ul of 100 uM ~150 ul of 100 uM > 500 ul of 100 uM I typically do not add pyrophosphatase until the reaction starts turning cloudy (after 30 minutes to 1 hour). This is a positive control that the transcription is working, and the PPIase then stops accumulation of PPI CRITICAL It is important to keep all reagents on ice but set up the reaction at room temperature in the order indicated . Cold spermidine (in the transcription buffer) can precipitate the template DNA.

steps.title: Incubate in a water bath or...
steps.instruction: Incubate in a water bath or air incubator at 37°C for 5 hours to overnight. Undetermined, Incubation 05:00:00, Incubation It is not recommended to use a heat block since the temperature fluctuates too much (and may exceed 37°C).

steps.title: After transcription, add 1µL RQ1 RNase-free...
steps.instruction: After transcription, add 1µL RQ1 RNase-free DNase per 100µL of transcription and continue to incubate at 37°C for 30 minutes. Undetermined, Incubation 00:30:00, Incubation

steps.title: End the transcription by adding half...
steps.instruction: End the transcription by adding half transcription volume of 2x denaturing loading dye (e.g. 50µL for a 100µL transcription). Using this reduced amount of loading dye facilitates loading more RNA into a gel and is sufficient to denature short RNAs. PAUSE RNA is stable in denaturing loading dye overnight if necessary - immediate purification is advised.

steps.title: Please select one of the following two Cleanup options
steps.instruction: Please select one of the following two Cleanup options: Cleanup option #1: bead cleanup (much faster, may purify truncated products or nucleotides) Cleanup option #2: Gel purify transcribed RNA (more difficult, higher purity)

steps.title: CRITICAL Incubate RNAclean or homemade SPRI...
steps.instruction: CRITICAL Incubate RNAclean or homemade SPRI beads at room temperature for ~1 hour to bring to RT. 01:00:00, Incubation

steps.title: Add 900µL 100% EtOH to the transcription
steps.instruction: Add 900µL 100% EtOH to the transcription. undetermined, 100% EtOH This and all following steps assume a 100µL transcription. Scale up accordingly if using a larger transcription.

steps.title: Add 500µL SPRI beads
steps.instruction: Add 500µL SPRI beads. undetermined, 500µL SPRI beads

steps.title: Immediately pipet ten times to mix
steps.instruction: Immediately pipet ten times to mix.

steps.title: Incubate 5 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate 5 minutes at room temperature. 00:05:00, Incubation

steps.title: Move tube to magnetic stand for...
steps.instruction: Move tube to magnetic stand for five minutes or until clear. 00:05:00, Magnetic stand

steps.title: Aspirate supernatant without disturbing beads
steps.instruction: Aspirate supernatant without disturbing beads.

steps.title: Keep tube on magnet, add 1mL 80% EtOH
steps.instruction: Keep tube on magnet, add 1mL 80% EtOH. (1/3) Use freshly prepared 80% EtOH as it is hygroscopic. undetermined, 80% EtOH

steps.title: Wait 1 minute
steps.instruction: Wait 1 minute. (1/3) 00:01:00, Wait

steps.title: Aspirate wash
steps.instruction: Aspirate wash. (1/3)

steps.title: Keep tube on magnet, add 1mL 80% EtOH
steps.instruction: Keep tube on magnet, add 1mL 80% EtOH. (2/3) undetermined, 80% EtOH

steps.title: Remove any residual ethanol
steps.instruction: Remove any residual ethanol.

steps.title: Air dry pellet on magnet for 5-10 minutes
steps.instruction: Air dry pellet on magnet for 5-10 minutes. 00:05:00, Air drying

steps.title: Remove from magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove from magnetic rack.

steps.title: Add 50µL water
steps.instruction: Add 50µL water. undetermined, Water

steps.title: Pipet 5 times to mix
steps.instruction: Pipet 5 times to mix.

steps.title: Return to magnetic rack, wait until clear
steps.instruction: Return to magnetic rack, wait until clear.

steps.title: Transfer supernatant to new tube - this contains your RNA
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant to new tube - this contains your RNA.

steps.title: It is recommended to measure the...
steps.instruction: It is recommended to measure the concentration of this using a qubit

steps.title: Keep tube on magnet, add 1mL 80% EtOH
steps.instruction: Keep tube on magnet, add 1mL 80% EtOH. (3/3) undetermined, 80% EtOH

steps.title: Wait 1 minute
steps.instruction: Wait 1 minute. (2/3) 00:01:00, Wait

steps.title: Wait 1 minute
steps.instruction: Wait 1 minute. (3/3) 00:01:00, Wait

steps.title: Aspirate wash
steps.instruction: Aspirate wash. (2/3)

steps.title: Aspirate wash
steps.instruction: Aspirate wash. (3/3)

steps.title: Pour a 7M urea, 10% 29:1...
steps.instruction: Pour a 7M urea, 10% 29:1 polyacrylamide denaturing gel of sufficient size to hold all of the transcription(s).

steps.title: Run gel until the xylene band...
steps.instruction: Run gel until the xylene band is midway through the gel (a few hours).

steps.title: Image gel using UV shadowing to visualize band
steps.instruction: Image gel using UV shadowing to visualize band. Band should be just above the xylene.

steps.title: Excise gel slice with razor blade...
steps.instruction: Excise gel slice with razor blade and place in Eppendorf tube (small txn) or conical tube (large txn).

steps.title: Crush gel slice with a P...
steps.instruction: Crush gel slice with a P 1000 tip or glass rod.

steps.title: Add enough gel elution buffer to...
steps.instruction: Add enough gel elution buffer to cover gel bits so they can move around when rocked (typically ~500µL per 100µL transcription).

steps.title: Freeze the resuspended gel bits for...
steps.instruction: Freeze the resuspended gel bits for 30 minutes on dry ice or at -80°C. 00:30:00, Freezing gel bits Undetermined, Freezing gel bits This fractures the gel bits and increases yield.

steps.title: PAUSE Rock overnight at 4°C
steps.instruction: PAUSE Rock overnight at 4°C. Undetermined, Rocking overnight

steps.title: For small scale transcription, filter the...
steps.instruction: For small scale transcription, filter the gel bits through a 0.45 micron filter. Use a P-1000 tip with the end cut off using a razor blade to collect all the gel bits into the filter. The SDS may precipitate during this process, especially if left on ice. If so, repeat filtration using a new filter.

steps.title: For large scale transcription, vortex the...
steps.instruction: For large scale transcription, vortex the gel bits and pour all of it onto a flip-top conical tube filter. Allow to vacuum filter until no more drops are retained.

steps.title: Ethanol precipitate the RNA by adding...
steps.instruction: Ethanol precipitate the RNA by adding 1/1000 volumes glycoblue and 2.5 volumes 100% ethanol.

steps.title: Incubate at -80°C for at least...
steps.instruction: Incubate at -80°C for at least 1 hour but note that RNA under EtOH is stable for months at -80°C. Undetermined, Incubation 01:00:00, Incubation

steps.title: Spin down at 16k g (small-scale)...
steps.instruction: Spin down at 16k g (small-scale) or 5k g (large-scale) for 25 minutes at 4°C. Undetermined, Spin down 00:25:00, Spin down For large-scale transcription, resuspend the pellet from above in 500µL of -20 degree 100% EtOH and transfer to a microfuge tube. The pellet will remain chunky - collect all the chunks with more ethanol if needed. Spin again at 16k g for 5 minutes to re-pellet.

steps.title: Aspirate supernatant and discard, being careful not to dislodge pellet
steps.instruction: Aspirate supernatant and discard, being careful not to dislodge pellet.

steps.title: Wash pellet with 500µL -20°C 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Wash pellet with 500µL -20°C 70% ethanol. undetermined, -20°C 70% ethanol CRITICAL Pipet the 70% ethanol gently not onto the pellet. Keep the tube cold to avoid resuspending the RNA in the 70% ethanol.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in ~30µL DEPC H...
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in ~30µL DEPC H 2 O per 100µL transcription and quantify using nanodrop or qubit.

steps.title: Spin at 16k g for 5 minutes at 4°C
steps.instruction: Spin at 16k g for 5 minutes at 4°C. Undetermined, Spin 00:05:00, Spin

steps.title: Aspirate supernatant carefully and discard
steps.instruction: Aspirate supernatant carefully and discard.

steps.title: Quick spin in tabletop centrifuge
steps.instruction: Quick spin in tabletop centrifuge.

steps.title: Aspirate remaining ethanol and discard
steps.instruction: Aspirate remaining ethanol and discard.

steps.title: Air dry at room temperature for...
steps.instruction: Air dry at room temperature for 5 minutes with the tube cap open. 00:05:00, Air drying
